children.name: Petroica
children.description: The scarlet robins — a genus of small, brilliantly coloured Australasian robins with strikingly bright red or pink breast patches set against dark upperparts. They are among the most familiar and beloved birds of Australia and New Zealand, with species occurring from tropical islands to subantarctic outposts. They are upright, active insectivores that perch conspicuously and drop onto prey.

children.name: Petroica boodang
children.description: A small, striking robin with the male an unmistakable combination of glossy black upperparts, a brilliant scarlet breast, and white belly, found in southeastern and southwestern Australia. It is a territorial, upright-perching insectivore that drops from low perches onto ground-dwelling prey. It is most common in open eucalypt forests and woodlands with a grassy understorey.

children.name: Petroica phoenicea
children.description: A robin with the male a brilliant flame-orange breast and throat contrasting with grey upperparts, found in southeastern Australia. It is a partial migrant, moving from higher-elevation breeding areas to lower woodlands and farmlands in winter. Its habit of perching on fence posts and dropping onto insects makes it a conspicuous and beloved bird in rural Australia.

children.name: Petroica rodinogaster
children.description: A small, delicate robin with the male a soft rose-pink breast and throat fading to white on the belly, with dark grey upperparts, found in the cool temperate rainforests of southeastern Australia and Tasmania. It is a shy, unobtrusive bird of the damp forest understorey, often seen perched quietly on a mossy branch. Its soft, high-pitched calls are more often heard than the bird is seen.

children.name: Petroica australis
children.description: A large robin endemic to New Zealand, with dark grey upperparts and pale underparts, known for its confiding nature.

children.name: Petroica traversi
children.description: A small endangered robin endemic to the Chatham Islands of New Zealand, saved from extinction by translocations.

children.name: Petroica macrocephala
children.description: A small active robin of New Zealand forests, with the male having a black head, white cap, and yellow breast.

children.name: Petroica goodenovii
children.description: A striking robin with glossy black upperparts, brilliant red cap, and white underparts, found in dry woodlands of inland Australia.

children.name: Petroica multicolor
children.description: A robin found on Norfolk Island and the southwest Pacific, with glossy black upperparts and scarlet breast.

children.name: Petroica pusilla
children.description: A small active robin with olive-brown upperparts and yellow underparts, found in eastern Australia and New Guinea.

children.name: Petroica vittata
children.description: A sombre olive-brown robin with a pale throat, found in Tasmania and the Bass Strait islands.

children.name: Eopsaltria
children.description: The yellow robins — a genus of small, plump Australasian robins with bright yellow underparts and olive-grey or grey upperparts, found in forests of Australia and New Guinea. They are among the most confiding and easily observed of all Australian passerines, often perching quietly at eye level in the forest understorey. They are monogamous and maintain permanent territories in pairs.

children.name: Eopsaltria australis
children.description: A plump, friendly robin with a bright yellow breast and belly, grey-olive back, and a habit of perching quietly on low branches in the forest understorey, common along the entire eastern coast of Australia. It is well known for its trusting nature, often approaching human observers within arm's reach. It forages by pouncing on ground-dwelling prey from a low perch, returning to the same perch repeatedly.

children.name: Eopsaltria georgiana
children.description: A robin with white underparts, grey upperparts, and a pale yellow patch on the belly, restricted to the forests of southwestern Australia. It is a quiet, unobtrusive bird that perches in the lower storey of jarrah, karri, and marri forests. Like other yellow robins, it uses a perch-and-pounce foraging technique, dropping onto insects on the forest floor.

children.name: Eopsaltria flaviventris
children.description: A plump robin with bright yellow underparts and olive-grey upperparts, found in northern Australia and southern New Guinea.

children.name: Eopsaltria pulverulenta
children.description: A pale grey robin with white underparts, restricted to mangroves of northern Australia and New Guinea.

children.name: Drymodes
children.description: The scrub robins — a genus of longer-legged, more terrestrial Australasian robins with a furtive, ground-dwelling habit. They have a distinctive upright stance and a preference for dense, dry thickets and spinifex grasslands. Their longer legs allow them to run swiftly through the undergrowth, and their subtle plumage provides excellent camouflage.

children.name: Drymodes brunneopygia
children.description: A large, long-legged robin with warm brown upperparts, a pale eyebrow, and a long tail that is often cocked, found in mallee shrublands and heathy woodlands of southern Australia. It is a secretive, ground-dwelling bird that runs swiftly through low scrub and spinifex, foraging for insects. Its thin, plaintive whistle is far easier to hear than the bird itself is to observe.

children.name: Drymodes superciliaris
children.description: A long-legged, ground-dwelling robin with brown upperparts and bold white eyebrow, found in New Guinea.

children.name: Microeca
children.description: The flycatcher-robins — a genus of small, active robins with a more slender build and flycatching foraging style.

children.name: Microeca fascinans
children.description: A small, pale grey-brown robin with white underparts and a habit of constantly flicking its wings, found across most of Australia in open woodlands.

children.name: Microeca flavigaster
children.description: A small robin with olive-grey upperparts and a bright yellow belly, found in northern Australia and New Guinea.

children.name: Microeca griseoceps
children.description: A tiny olive-grey robin with pale throat and buff underparts, found in lowland forests of New Guinea.

children.name: Microeca flavovirescens
children.description: A small olive-green robin with pale yellow underparts, found in lowland forests of the Aru Islands and southern New Guinea.

children.name: Microeca hemixantha
children.description: A small robin with olive upperparts and bright yellow belly, found in the lowland forests of the Moluccas.

children.name: Microeca papuana
children.description: A small olive-brown robin with pale underparts, found in the montane forests of New Guinea.

children.name: Heteromyias
children.description: The ashy robins — medium-sized robins with grey or brown upperparts and white underparts.

children.name: Heteromyias albispecularis
children.description: A medium-sized robin with grey upperparts and white underparts, found in the montane forests of New Guinea.

children.name: Heteromyias cinereifrons
children.description: A robin with a pale grey face, brown upperparts, and white underparts, found in the rainforests of northeastern Queensland.

children.name: Heteromyias armiti
children.description: A medium-sized robin with black cheeks and white underparts, found in the montane forests of New Guinea.

children.name: Peneothello
children.description: The slate robins — dark grey robins found in the forests of New Guinea.

children.name: Peneothello cryptoleuca
children.description: A dark grey robin with a contrasting white rump, found in the montane forests of New Guinea.

children.name: Peneothello cyanus
children.description: A uniform slate-grey robin with blackish wings, found in the montane forests of New Guinea.

children.name: Peneothello bimaculata
children.description: A dark grey robin with distinctive white wing patches, found in the lowland forests of New Guinea.

children.name: Peneothello sigillata
children.description: A dark slate-grey robin with a pure white breast, found in the montane forests of New Guinea.

children.name: Poecilodryas
children.description: A genus of medium-sized robins with white eyebrows and bold patterns, found in Australia and New Guinea.

children.name: Poecilodryas cerviniventris
children.description: A robin with dark brown upperparts, bold white eyebrow, and buff underparts, found in lowland forests of northern Australia and New Guinea.

children.name: Poecilodryas brachyura
children.description: A robin with olive-brown upperparts, black chin, and white underparts, found in lowland forests of New Guinea.

children.name: Poecilodryas hypoleuca
children.description: A black-and-white robin with glossy black upperparts and white wing patches, found in lowland forests of New Guinea.

children.name: Poecilodryas superciliosa
children.description: A robin with dark brown upperparts, bold white eyebrow, and white underparts, found in lowland forests of northeastern Australia.

children.name: Poecilodryas albonotata
children.description: A robin with dark grey upperparts, black throat, and white underparts, found in the montane forests of New Guinea.

children.name: Melanodryas
children.description: A genus of striking black-and-white robins found in Australia.

children.name: Melanodryas cucullata
children.description: A striking robin with glossy black upperparts, white underparts, and white wing patches, found in dry woodlands across much of Australia.

children.name: Melanodryas vittata
children.description: A sombre olive-brown robin with a pale throat and belly, endemic to Tasmania and the Bass Strait islands.

children.name: Tregellasia
children.description: A genus of small, plump robins with pale yellow underparts, found in Australia and New Guinea.

children.name: Tregellasia capito
children.description: A small, plump robin with olive-green upperparts and pale yellow underparts, found in the rainforests of eastern Australia.

children.name: Tregellasia leucops
children.description: A small robin with olive-green upperparts, white face, and yellow underparts, found in the lowland forests of New Guinea.

children.name: Gennaeodryas
children.description: A monotypic genus of olive-yellow robin from New Guinea.

children.name: Gennaeodryas placens
children.description: A small olive-yellow robin with a pale throat, found in the montane forests of New Guinea.

children.name: Eugerygone
children.description: A monotypic genus of small robin with bright red back, from New Guinea.

children.name: Eugerygone rubra
children.description: A small robin with the male having a bright crimson back, found in the montane forests of New Guinea.

children.name: Pachycephalopsis
children.description: A genus of medium-sized green-backed robins from New Guinea.

children.name: Pachycephalopsis hattamensis
children.description: A medium-sized robin with olive-green upperparts and yellow underparts, found in the lowland forests of New Guinea.

children.name: Pachycephalopsis poliosoma
children.description: A medium-sized robin with olive-green upperparts and a distinctive pale eye, found in the montane forests of New Guinea.

children.name: Monachella
children.description: A monotypic genus of torrent robin from New Guinea.

children.name: Monachella muelleriana
children.description: A striking black-and-white robin found along fast-flowing streams in the montane forests of New Guinea.

children.name: Poecilodryas
children.description: Robins from Australia and New Guinea.

children.name: Poecilodryas cerviniventris
children.description: A robin from northern Australia and New Guinea.

children.name: Poecilodryas superciliosa
children.description: A robin from northeastern Australia.

children.name: Poecilodryas albonotata
children.description: A robin from the montane forests of New Guinea.

children.name: Poecilodryas brachyura
children.description: Poecilodryas brachyura (Black-chinned Robin) is a australasian robins species found in Asia region.

children.name: Poecilodryas hypoleuca
children.description: Poecilodryas hypoleuca (White-winged Robin) is a australasian robins species found in Asia region.

children.name: Melanodryas
children.description: Striking black-and-white robins from Australia.

children.name: Melanodryas cucullata
children.description: A striking robin with glossy black upperparts and white underparts.

children.name: Melanodryas vittata
children.description: A sombre robin endemic to Tasmania.
